reasoning: Bitcoin needs to fall to $90,000, representing a 27.4% drawdown from its $124,000 peak. In the post-ETF era, Bitcoin's major corrections have typically plateaued around 20-25%. The recent low of $99,966 on Nov 4 represents a ~20% drawdown, suggesting that the "ETF-era average" correction might already be complete. However, the $100,000 level is in a "fragile equilibrium." If it fails, thin liquidity down to $93,000 leaves Bitcoin vulnerable to a liquidation cascade, and the "1-minute lowest price" criteria on Binance makes flash wicks touching ≤$90,000 possible. Standard options models (GBM) assuming 45-55% volatility over the remaining 52 days give a ~40-50% probability of touching a barrier ~10-12% away. Factoring in strong Q4 bullish seasonality and positive drift towards consensus targets of $150k+, the probability is slightly diminished. Balancing the completed 20% drawdown against the risk of thin-liquidity wicks, I estimate 42%.
